title: Bi-directional perfusion cannula
abstract: A bi-directional perfusion cannula comprising a flexible elongated tube 1 for insertion into an artery having a first aperture 3 at a distal end and a second aperture 4 formed rearward of the first aperture, the elongated tube can be bent in correspondence of the second aperture to orient it in a direction opposite to the insertion direction. The cannula is provided with a tape 5 having a first end portion shaped as a ring, fixed on an outer surface of the elongated tube, and a second end portion opposite to the first end portion either coupled to or being integral with a belt 7 defining a strap 11 with holes 12 and at least a prong 13 integral with the strap and configured to engage a respective hole, wherein the belt is configured to be tightened around an outer surface of the artery.
claims:
Claims 1. A bi-directional perfusion cannula, comprising a flexible elongated tube (1) for insertion into an artery (2) configured to lay longitudinally in the artery (2), having: a first aperture (3) oriented forward during insertion into an artery (2) at a first end of a distal portion of the elongated tube (1), said distal portion of the elongated tube (1) being configured to lay longitudinally in the artery (2), said first aperture (3) being configured so that blood can flow into the artery (2) in a direction of insertion, a second aperture (4) formed rearward of the first aperture (3) at a second end of said distal portion of the elongated tube (1), and configured to supply blood into the artery (2) in a second direction, said elongated tube (1) being configured to be bent in correspondence of said second aperture (4) in order to orient said second aperture (4) in said second direction opposite to the insertion direction, characterized in that the bi-directional perfusion cannula comprises: a tape (5) having a first end portion (6) shaped as a ring, fixed on an outer surface of the elongated tube (1), and a second end portion (10) opposite to the first end portion (6), said second end portion (10) being either configured to be coupled to or being integral with a belt (7) defining a strap (11) with holes (12) and at least a prong (13) integral with said strap (11) and configured to engage a respective one of said holes (12), said belt (7) being made of a bio-compatible material and being configured to be tightened around an outer surface of the artery (2); wherein said tape (5) is configured to allow to tighten said belt (7) at a distance from a point of insertion of said elongated tube (1) in the artery (2), so as to tighten the artery (2) together with the distal portion of the elongated tube (1) when said distal portion of the elongated tube (1) lays longitudinally in the artery (2). 2. The bi-directional perfusion cannula of claim 1, wherein said belt (7) being integral with a head (8) with a pawl (9), configured to engage with teeth of said second end portion (10); said second end portion (10) is configured to be coupled with said belt (7) and has teeth configured to engage with the pawl (9) of said head (8) to form a ratchet as a cable 

 tie. 3. The bi-directional perfusion cannula of claim 1, comprising a metal wire (14) incorporated into the flexible elongated tube (1) as a spiral, wherein said spiral has an augmented step in correspondence of said second aperture (4). 4. The bi-directional perfusion cannula of one of claims from 1 to 3, comprising a plurality of prongs (13) integral with said strap (11). 5. The bi-directional perfusion cannula of one of claims from 1 to 4, wherein said second aperture (4) has a sectional area up 50% of a corresponding sectional area of said first aperture (3). 6. The bi-directional perfusion cannula of claim 5, wherein said second aperture (4) has a sectional area ranging between 25% and 30% of a corresponding sectional area of said first aperture (3).
